svg.text: One wire to GPIO pin · one wire to GND · no external resistors (ESP32 internal pull-ups)
Generic Input Circuit (same for every switch)
Run 2 wires from protoboard terminals to each switch:
Switches do not need to be polarized — any SPST momentary or toggle works.
new — terminal only, no HTTP action yet
SW4/SW5 already exist — add SW6–SW11 terminal blocks in same row to keep neat.
SW9–SW11 spare inputs: ESPHome shows state but no HTTP POST fires — assign later.
⚠ GPIO5 and GPIO14 are ESP32 strapping pins — internal pull-ups keep them HIGH at boot (correct).
Mount a single 16-position (or two 8-position) screw terminal strips in a row · one pair per switch
GND bus (all T2 terminals connected to this rail)
GPIO wires run from ESP32 pin header to T1 of each switch pair
one pair per new switch (SW6–SW11) · or use one 12-pin strip cut to length
signal wires (green/yellow) + GND wires (black) from terminals to switch panel
No resistors needed for switch inputs
ESP32 internal pull-ups are enabled in ESPHome (mode: input, pullup: true)
⚠ All T2 terminals share a single GND — daisy-chain all T2s together on the GND rail or use a shared bus bar.
⚠ SW4/SW5 already wired to GPIO32/33 — match the same T1=signal / T2=GND convention when adding new terminals.
